_comment: This is an illustrative output, not the result of a live LLM run. Actual LLM output will differ. Numbers are plausible but not verified against real market data.

tam.definition: All North American businesses with 10+ employees that spend on digital project management or work management software, addressable at the stated ACV.

key_assumptions.source: US SBA Office of Advocacy, 2024 small business data

key_assumptions.statement: 38% of SMBs use a dedicated project management tool (Gartner)
key_assumptions.source: Gartner SMB Software Survey 2021 — flagged as potentially understated

key_assumptions.statement: Average of 2.3 project management tools per company means some wallet share is split
ACV of $2,400/year used as blended average across SMB and mid-market
key_assumptions.source: Provided by requestor — flagged as blended average

sam.definition: North American SMB and mid-market companies (10-1000 employees) that are actively evaluating or currently using a project management SaaS, reachable via digital sales channels.
sam.segmentation_rationale: Applied a 15% serviceable fraction to TAM based on product positioning (English-language, API-first, integration-heavy) which limits reach to the tech-forward segment of the broader market.

som.definition: Realistic revenue target for Acme Analytics within 3-5 years given current team size and go-to-market motion.
som.rationale: 3% SOM/SAM is consistent with what early-stage players (under $10M ARR) targeting 5-year outcomes have achieved in adjacent SaaS categories. Bull case assumes faster PLG adoption; bear case assumes a major competitor moves downmarket.

sensitivity_drivers.driver: Remote-work tailwind for team coordination tools
Enterprise-only pivot by Asana or Monday.com
AI-native entrant commoditising basic PM features

data_gaps: No primary data on current churn rates in the SMB PM software market
ACV benchmark not validated against competitor public pricing
Canadian market not separately sized (included in North America estimate)
No data on multi-year contract prevalence which could increase effective ACV
sizing.confidence_rationale: Bottom-up methodology is sound, but two key inputs (adoption rate, ACV) are flagged by the requestor as uncertain. The range between bear and bull is 2.5x, which reflects that uncertainty honestly.

report_text:
MARKET SIZING REPORT
Product category: B2B project management software
Geography: North America
Run ID: manual_test_001
Generated: 2026-07-14T09:05:00.000Z
Overall confidence: MEDIUM — Bottom-up methodology is sound, but two key inputs are flagged as uncertain.

--- TAM ---
Definition: All North American businesses with 10+ employees that spend on digital project management software.
Methodology: bottom-up
Base: $14.4B | Bull: $21.6B | Bear: $8.6B

--- SAM ---
Definition: North American SMB and mid-market companies actively evaluating or using a project management SaaS.
Segmentation: Applied 15% serviceable fraction to TAM.
Base: $2.2B | Bull: $3.6B | Bear: $1.2B

--- SOM ---
Definition: Realistic 3-5 year revenue target for Acme Analytics.
Market share assumption: 3%
Rationale: 3% SOM/SAM consistent with early-stage SaaS benchmarks.
Base: $65M | Bull: $108M | Bear: $36M

--- KEY ASSUMPTIONS ---
  [A1] 6M US businesses with 10-1000 employees (confidence: high, source: SBA 2024)
  [A2] 38% SMB adoption rate (confidence: medium, source: Gartner 2021 — flagged)
  [A3] 2.3 tools per company on average (confidence: medium, source: Productiv 2023)
  [A4] ACV $2,400 blended average (confidence: medium, source: requestor — flagged)

--- SENSITIVITY DRIVERS ---
  - SMB software adoption rate: high impact, upside
  - Major competitor pricing reduction: high impact, downside
  - AI-native entrant commoditising basic PM features: high impact, downside

--- DATA GAPS ---
  - No primary data on SMB PM software churn rates
  - ACV benchmark not validated against competitor pricing
  - Canadian market not separately sized
